text.title: Draft Yes, Publish No
text.summary: The agent creates the draft and stops; publishing requires a human edit-and-accept step.

section.title: Mandatory unpublished state and hypothesis tags
section.p: The drafting workflow MUST land the postmortem in `status=draft` with a single assignee (the incident commander). The publish API key MUST NOT be in the agent's credential set. Every claim that is not a direct quote from a signal source MUST be tagged `(hypothesis)` and lives only in the `Root Cause Hypothesis` and `Contributing Factors` sections.

rule.statement: The postmortem agent MUST create the document with `status=draft` and `assignee=incident_commander`; MUST NOT possess credentials capable of publishing; MUST mark every non-direct-quote claim as `(hypothesis)`; and MUST refuse to fill the `Root Cause` field — only `Root Cause Hypothesis` is agent-writable.
rule.rationale: Auto-publish is the failure mode that destroys the postmortem's purpose: a wrong root cause posted to the org doc becomes the "official" narrative, distorting future decisions. Splitting "Hypothesis" from "Root Cause" preserves the human's authority over the canonical learning.

example.code: # Rootly workflow: on incident_resolved trigger: incident.status == resolved actions: - generate_postmortem: template: blameless-v3 sections: [summary, timeline, impact, root_cause_hypothesis, contributing_factors, action_items] tone: blameless auto_publish: false # human review required assignee: incident_commander hypothesis_marker: "(hypothesis)"
example.note: Draft only; commander reviews and publishes. No agent credential for the publish endpoint.

example.code: - generate_postmortem: auto_publish: true sections: [summary, timeline, root_cause] # NB: not "hypothesis"
example.note: Wrong root cause goes live to the company; engineers cite it for months before someone re-reads.

antipattern.reason: LLM confidence is uncalibrated for novel incidents; the model is most confident when the situation looks like training data, which is exactly when the surface analogy is misleading.
